messages.text: In the desert, the little prince explained that he had not always lived alone with volcanoes and a routine. One morning a new shoot began to appear unlike the baobabs.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: I watched it carefully. It took a long time to prepare itself. A flower should not cause so much suspense.
At last the rose opened, delicate and bright, as if sunrise had chosen to live on his planet. She looked at him and yawned.

messages.name: Rose
messages.text: I believe it is breakfast time. Would you be kind enough to think of me?
He found water at once. Her beauty made him happy, but her pride soon made every simple task feel like a test.

messages.name: Rose
messages.text: My petals are strong, but I dislike drafts. A screen would protect me. At night I need a glass shelter.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: I brought the screen, and I brought the shelter. Are you comfortable now?

messages.name: Rose
messages.text: As comfortable as one can be on such a windy planet. I come from a place with tigers, you know.
There were no tigers there, and roses have thorns for only a small defense. Still, he believed her because he wanted to believe.
Her words were vain, but not cruel. She was young, afraid, and too proud to show a sincere need plainly.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: I should have judged her by what she did, not only by what she said. She gave scent and color to my life.
At the time, however, every cough and complaint became a conflict. He felt accused, confused, and suddenly eager to escape his own home.

messages.name: Rose
messages.text: You are leaving? Then go. I was foolish. Try to be happy. Do not cover me with the glass tonight.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: But the wind will come.

messages.name: Rose
messages.text: I must endure a few caterpillars if I want to meet butterflies.
Her courage sounded almost sincere, and that made his departure harder. He cleaned the volcanoes one last time and pulled the final baobab shoots.
The routine of care became farewell. The little planet looked too small for his sadness and too large for one rose left alone.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: Goodbye.

messages.name: Rose
messages.text: Goodbye. I love you. You did not know it. That was my fault.
He left by a passing flock of wild birds, carrying confusion, pride, and a love he did not yet understand.
The rose had filled his mornings with fragrance before she filled them with anxiety. Even her vanity had arrived wrapped in color and perfume.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: When she coughed, I thought she was blaming me. Now I think she only wanted me to stay near.
He was too young to understand that pride can be a poor shelter for fear. She was too proud to ask plainly for tenderness.

messages.name: Rose
messages.text: Do not linger over goodbye. If I let you see me cry, you will think I am ordinary.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: I did not know how to answer. I wanted to protect her, but I also wanted to escape the conflict.
So he left with love disguised as irritation. The birds lifted him away while the glass shelter stood unused in the morning light.
While he spoke, I realized that leaving is sometimes easier than learning another heart. The rose had asked for shelter, but he had needed guidance too.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: I knew how to clean volcanoes. I did not know how to answer a flower.
The more he remembered, the more the rose changed in his telling. She was no longer only vain; she was delicate, proud, afraid, and beloved.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: I left because I was hurt, but I think she was hurt first.
